name: Hypothyroidism - Abnormal TSH Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find hypothyroidism patients via abnormal Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) values. The PheKB algorithm defines a case threshold of TSH > 5 mIU/L. This test exercises Observation queries with value-quantity filters. Returns Path A patients (with full presentation) and Path D tricky patients (abnormal labs only, never treated).
prompt: Find laboratory observations for thyroid stimulating hormone tests where the result was greater than 5 mIU/L, indicating possible hypothyroidism.

prompts.naive: Find patients with high TSH lab values.
prompts.broad: Find lab observations for thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH, also called thyrotropin) where the value is above the upper limit of normal (typically > 5 milli-international units per liter), which suggests primary hypothyroidism.
prompts.expert: I need to find Observation resources for elevated TSH. The standard LOINC code for TSH in serum or plasma is 3016-3 (Thyrotropin:ACnc:Pt:Ser/Plas:Qn). The PheKB hypothyroidism algorithm uses a threshold of TSH > 5 mIU/L. Use a value-quantity filter with the gt prefix and unit mIU/L. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L. Note that some FHIR servers accept simpler unit notation.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated TSH (Paths A and D). Includes Path D tricky patients who have abnormal labs but were never given a hypothyroidism diagnosis or replacement therapy.
